# cascivo — The CSS-native, signal-driven, AI-first React design system

cascivo is a React design system you can consume two ways: copy-paste the source
(`npx cascivo add <name>` — shadcn model, you own the code) or install the prebuilt
`@cascivo/react` package (use without copying). Components are signal-driven (Preact
Signals), styled with modern CSS (@layer / @container / :has() / oklch), themed via the
`data-theme` attribute, and WCAG 2.2 AA. Every component ships a machine-readable manifest,
so this file plus the linked resources are enough to build with cascivo.

This is not a layout-only system: it includes 16 overlay/menu components
(dropdown, menu, context menu, combobox, command palette, multi-select) with keyboard
navigation, focus management, and outside-click dismissal already implemented — do not
hand-roll that behavior or reach for a separate headless library. It also ships 25
chart types (`@cascivo/charts` — line, area, bar, sparkline, KPI, heatmap, and more) and
22 pre-built page blocks (dashboards, stats cards, app shells) so a request like
"build a dashboard with usage sparklines and a project switcher" maps to existing parts —
see `select_component`/`list_components` below before generating custom SVG or ARIA code.

## Start here

- **Everything in ONE file (no follow-up fetches — fetch this if you can only fetch once):** https://cascivo.com/llms-full.txt
- Getting started, fetchable as plain markdown (install steps, no browser needed): https://cascivo.com/docs/getting-started.md
- Docs (per-component reference, props, live examples): https://docs.cascivo.com
- Storybook (every variant, interactive): https://storybook.cascivo.com
- Source (MIT): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo
- This file: https://cascivo.com/llms.txt (mirrored at https://docs.cascivo.com/llms.txt)

## Machine-readable resources (AI-first)

- Full text bundle — this file with every component reference inlined (single fetch): https://cascivo.com/llms-full.txt
- Registry — index of every component (JSON): https://cascivo.com/registry.json
- shadcn/v0-compatible registry (install with the shadcn CLI or "Open in v0"): https://cascivo.com/r/shadcn/registry.json
- Per-component AI docs (props, examples, a11y, tokens): https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/<name>.md
- Page-block AI docs are namespaced: https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/<name>.md
- A wrong/guessed name under /llms/*, /context/*, or /r/* returns HTTP 404 with a short hint
  body (not the HTML SPA) — trust the 404, then look up the real name in the index below.
- Per-component context (intent, when-to-use, boundaries): https://docs.cascivo.com/context/<name>.md
- Context bundle (all intent + boundaries + rules): https://docs.cascivo.com/context.json
- Token catalog (closed set, every --cascivo-* + layer + default): https://docs.cascivo.com/tokens.catalog.json
- Icon catalog (every @cascivo/icons icon + keywords/category): https://docs.cascivo.com/icons.catalog.json
- Breaking/feature changes (major+minor releases per package — detect API drift): https://docs.cascivo.com/breaking-changes.json

## Icons

443 stroked 24×24 `currentColor` SVG icons ship as tree-shakeable named exports
from `@cascivo/icons` (e.g. `import { Camera, Anchor } from '@cascivo/icons'`).
Browse and search them at https://docs.cascivo.com/icons; the full machine-readable list (names,
keywords, aliases, categories) is https://docs.cascivo.com/icons.catalog.json.

Names differ from Lucide/Radix — resolve intent via the catalog `keywords` + `aliases` fields
rather than guessing an export name (a wrong guess is a wasted compile).
Common mappings: LayoutDashboard→Dashboard, Rocket→Spaceship, Settings2→Settings, Trash2→Trash, Bolt→Zap, Branch→GitBranch. Version-control icons exist:
`GitBranch`, `GitCommit`, `GitMerge`, `GitPullRequest`.

Pure-CSS glyphs (experimental): a small opt-in set of UI glyphs, rendered with
`clip-path: shape()` and no SVG — color via `currentColor`, size via
`--cascivo-glyph-size`, plus zero-JS state morphs via `transition: clip-path`.
Use `import { Glyph } from '@cascivo/icons'` with `import '@cascivo/icons/glyphs.css'`,
or the class form `<span class="cascivo-glyph" data-glyph="chevron-down">`. Glyphs:
`chevron-{down,up,left,right}`, `x`, `check`, `plus`, `minus`, `menu`,
`arrow-{right,left,up,down}`, and the `chevron-toggle` morph. Baseline-2026 only
(Chrome 135+, Safari 18.4+, Firefox 148+) — painting is gated behind `@supports`;
prefer the SVG icons for a wider browser floor or for print.

## How to use it

Two consumption paths — they share the same tokens/themes and can coexist:

A. Prebuilt package — fastest, use without copying source:

```sh
pnpm add @cascivo/react @cascivo/themes @preact/signals-react
```

```tsx
import '@cascivo/react/styles.css' // component styles (structure only)
import '@cascivo/themes/all'       // tokens (once) + base typography + light & dark
import { Button, Card } from '@cascivo/react'
```

   CSS note: with a bundler (CSR) per-component CSS auto-includes and tree-shakes, so the
   `@cascivo/react/styles.css` line above is optional; under Vite SSR / TanStack Start it is
   required (see SSR SETUP below) and ships the full sheet (~273 KB / ~37 KB gzip).

   Dashboards / data viz: charts are a SEPARATE install — `pnpm add @cascivo/charts`, then
   `import { AreaChart } from '@cascivo/charts'`. `@cascivo/react` exports no charts. The code
   editor (`@cascivo/editor`) and flow canvas (`@cascivo/flow`) are separate installs too.

   Trade-offs: smallest setup, updates via npm; you do not edit component internals.

B. Copy-paste source — own and customize the code (shadcn model):

```sh
npx cascivo add button card
```

   Runtime deps for copied source: `@cascivo/core`, `@cascivo/tokens`, `@cascivo/themes`, and the
   `@preact/signals-react` peer. `cascivo init` installs all of them (plus `cascivo` as a dev dep
   for the generated config); `cascivo add` installs any extra a component declares (e.g. @cascivo/i18n).

   Trade-offs: full control over internals; updates are a manual re-add.

Required CSS import order (both paths): components -> tokens+theme -> brand overrides (last).
`@cascivo/react/styles.css` is structure-only and needs a theme + tokens for color.

Charts, the code editor, and flow ship their own stylesheet: when you use them, import the
matching CSS once too — `import '@cascivo/charts/styles.css'` (likewise
`@cascivo/editor/styles.css`, `@cascivo/flow/styles.css`). Skipping the charts stylesheet is a
common mistake: the chart's screen-reader data-table fallback then renders visibly.

SSR SETUP (Vite SSR / TanStack Start / Remix / workerd — READ THIS if you server-render): the
prebuilt package imports per-component CSS as static `.css` side-effect imports, which a bare
server ESM loader cannot load — an unconfigured SSR build throws `Unknown file extension ".css"`
and silently falls back to client-only. Two required steps:
  1. In vite.config: `ssr: { noExternal: [/^@cascivo\//] }` (or add the `cascivoSsr()`
     plugin from `@cascivo/vite-plugin`) so Vite processes the CSS instead of the runtime.
  2. Import `@cascivo/react/styles.css` once in the root route/entry (do NOT rely on the
     per-component imports server-side). Full recipe: USING-WITH-VITE-SSR.md.
Next.js App Router does NOT need this (its recipe imports the aggregate sheet in a Server
Component); plain Vite CSR/SPA does NOT need it either. Only Vite *SSR* runtimes do.

C. shadcn CLI / v0 — install any component from the shadcn-compatible registry:

```sh
npx shadcn@latest add https://cascivo.com/r/shadcn/button.json
```

Every registry item inlines its own source and lists its cascivo dependencies as absolute
`https://cascivo.com/r/shadcn/<name>.json` URLs, so the shadcn CLI resolves them transitively. To add
cascivo as a named registry in `components.json` (then `npx shadcn add @cascivo/<name>`):

```json
{ "registries": { "@cascivo": "https://cascivo.com/r/shadcn/{name}.json" } }
```

The registry index (all items) is https://cascivo.com/r/shadcn/registry.json.

## CSS layer contract (follow when generating or editing CSS)

cascivo styles live in cascade layers. Layer order beats selector specificity, so:

1. Every declaration goes inside an `@layer` block. Unlayered CSS beats all layers regardless
   of specificity — never emit it.
2. Never invent layer names. The only layers you write: your app slot (e.g. `cascivo.example`,
   declared in your order statement) for page styles, and `@layer cascivo.override { … }` for
   hotfixes/one-off overrides — it beats everything cascivo ships.
3. Never nest layers deeper than the shipped `cascivo.blocks.<name>` pattern. For sub-elements
   (a badge in a card, a dot in a badge) use native CSS nesting inside one layer block, not new
   sublayers.
4. Third-party CSS: `@import url(…) layer(vendor);` with `vendor` declared before the cascivo
   layers. Never import vendor CSS from JavaScript.
5. Style with `--cascivo-*` tokens, not raw values.

Canonical order (lowest → highest priority):
`@layer vendor, cascivo.reset, cascivo.base, cascivo.tokens, cascivo.component, cascivo.theme, cascivo.blocks, cascivo.override;`
Full recipe: https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/blob/main/docs/CSS-LAYERS-PITFALL.md.

## Reactivity & state (signals, not React hooks)

**Consuming components requires no signals.** Pass plain props, use `useState` in YOUR own code,
and render `<Button onClick={…}>` / `<Modal open={…}>` like any React library — you never need
`useSignals()` unless your own component body reads a `signal.value` during render. Signals are
how cascivo works INTERNALLY; they are not a precondition for using it.

The rules below apply when you OWN reactive state — authoring a cascivo-style component, or opting
into cascivo's reactive store. There, prefer signals over React hooks: mixing the two (mirroring a
signal into `useState`, or vice-versa) causes the "state fights the DOM / toggle does nothing"
bugs. And in any app WITHOUT the Babel signals transform (every consumer app), a component that
reads `signal.value` in render MUST call `useSignals()` (from `@cascivo/core`) as its first
statement, or it never re-renders. When you do own reactive state, use these:

- Local state -> `useSignal(initial)`; derived -> `useComputed(fn)`. The signal IS the state.
- Controlled/uncontrolled prop bridged to a signal -> `useControllableSignal({ value, defaultValue, onChange })`.
- Side effects (DOM, listeners) -> `useSignalEffect(fn)`, never `useEffect`.
- Shared/app-wide state -> a module-level signal, imported anywhere. No React context needed.
- Per-route / per-tenant effect disposal -> `useScope()` / `createScope()` (disposes on unmount; prevents signal leaks across navigation).
- Genuine internal state machine -> `createMachine` / `useMachine`.
- Forms -> `createForm` / `useForm` / `<Form>` / `field()` (`@cascivo/react`): a signal-backed store with sync/async + Standard Schema (zod/valibot) validation and optional `validateOnChange` — keystroke validation, zero re-renders.
- Theming -> `<ThemeProvider>` + `useTheme()` / `setTheme()` + `themePreloadScript()` (`@cascivo/react`): persists the choice and drives `data-theme`, SSR no-FOUC. Never write a `useEffect` that toggles a `.dark` class.
- Token names in TypeScript -> `import type { CascivoToken, CascivoColorToken } from '@cascivo/tokens/tokens'` (generated union of every `--cascivo-*` property — no CSS-file lookup).

Full behavior/headless catalog: https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/blob/main/docs/HEADLESS.md. Enterprise friction -> primitive map: https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/blob/main/docs/ENTERPRISE-READINESS.md.

## Versioning & compatibility

Packages version independently (changesets) — a low number on one package does not mean the
whole system is behind. The compatibility truth is per-entry: every registry entry carries a
`peerVersions` floor (e.g. `{ "@cascivo/i18n": ">=0.2.1" }`) for the packages its copied source
needs. Pin exact versions, and after adding components run `cascivo doctor --drift` to catch an
installed peer that is older than a copied component needs. Watch https://docs.cascivo.com/breaking-changes.json
(major + minor releases per package) to detect API drift before upgrading.

## Reference apps (runnable, in-repo — copy these setups)

- Vite + React (CSR/SPA): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/tree/main/apps/examples/react-vite
- Vite SSR (TanStack Start / Remix / workerd — server-renders through the built dist with `cascivoSsr()`): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/tree/main/apps/examples/react-vite-ssr
- Next.js App Router (RSC): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/tree/main/apps/examples/react-next

## Frameworks & browsers (summary; full matrix in COMPATIBILITY.md)

- React 18/19, Next.js App Router (RSC), Vite (CSR), Astro islands, Preact 10 (preact/compat — verified).
- Vite SSR / TanStack Start / Remix / workerd: supported, but add `ssr.noExternal: [/^@cascivo\//]`
  (or the `cascivoSsr()` plugin) + import `@cascivo/react/styles.css` once. Without it the server
  loader throws `Unknown file extension ".css"`. See USING-WITH-VITE-SSR.md.
- Last 2 versions of Chrome/Firefox/Safari (requires @layer, @container, :has(), oklch()).
- CSS @function is opt-in (`import '@cascivo/tokens/functions.css'`); not auto-loaded, so
  lightningcss / Tailwind v4 pipelines work by default.

## MCP server (recommended for agents)

Point any MCP client at the cascivo server:

```json
{ "mcpServers": { "cascivo": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@cascivo/mcp"] } } }
```

Tools:
- `list_components` — list all components with category/tags
- `get_component` — full manifest for one component
- `get_context` — intent + boundaries + tokens for one component
- `get_tokens` — closed-set token catalog (filter by group/layer)
- `search_icons` — resolve an icon by intent/foreign name (LayoutDashboard→Dashboard)
- `select_component` — rank components by a natural-language need
- `scaffold_view` — natural language -> JSON view config
- `validate_view` — validate a view config against the schema
- `add_to_project` — install components into the user project

To pick a component without MCP, read the intent summaries below (grouped by need) or
fetch https://docs.cascivo.com/context/<name>.md for the full when-to-use / when-not-to-use of each.

## Component authoring rules (for cascivo:extend / custom components)

These constrain code you write INSIDE a component you author or copy-paste-and-edit — not the app
code that merely renders cascivo components (that code uses React normally).

- Signals only: `useSignal`, `useComputed`, `useSignalEffect` from `@cascivo/core`
- No `useState`, `useEffect`, `useContext`, `useLayoutEffect`, `useReducer`
- DOM side effects via `useSignalEffect`, not `useEffect`
- `useRef` only for DOM element references
- CSS custom properties only — no Tailwind, no inline styles, no CSS-in-JS
- Visual states (hover/focus/active) via CSS pseudo-classes — not JS
- `data-state` attributes only for states CSS cannot express (e.g. loading, error)
- FSM (`useMachine`) only when the component itself drives transitions
- CSS logical properties throughout (RTL-safe): `margin-inline-start`, etc.
- User-visible strings via `@cascivo/i18n` — no hardcoded English fallbacks
- WCAG 2.2 AA minimum — keyboard navigable, screen-reader tested

## High-throughput / streaming data

For live logs, build output, market ticks, or streamed tokens, do NOT rebuild an array per
event (`arr = [...arr.slice(1), line]` is O(n) per line and thrashes rendering). Use the
built-in primitives, which coalesce bursts to one render per frame:

- `useStreamBuffer` / `createStreamBuffer` (`@cascivo/core`) — fixed-capacity O(1) ring buffer,
  rAF-coalesced; a burst within one frame is a single signal write.
- `useStreamSeries` / `bindStream` (`@cascivo/charts`) — feed a live source into a chart with
  optional LTTB/min-max decimation so a fast stream never blows the rendered point count.
- `LogViewer` (`@cascivo/react`) — virtualized console for high-frequency logs (100k-line buffer
  stays responsive), ANSI parsing, auto-follow tail. Pairs with `createStreamBuffer`.

## Feedback signals for agents

cascivo ships no runtime telemetry (copy-paste model — you own the code; no phone-home). To
see how components behave without adding tracking, read these machine-readable CI outputs:

- Breaking/feature changes per package: https://docs.cascivo.com/breaking-changes.json
- Benchmark results (runtime, bundle, render counts vs Carbon/shadcn): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/blob/main/apps/bench/results/results.json
- Accessibility conformance (axe sweep, AT matrix): https://github.com/cascivo/cascivo/tree/main/docs/specs

## Component index (204 entries)

Not all ship in `@cascivo/react`: 25 in @cascivo/charts, 10 in @cascivo/flow, 2 in @cascivo/editor — those are separate installs (see Quick Setup). Blocks are copy-paste only. Each entry below is tagged with its channel.

Naming note: `Stack` overlaps children into a card-pile (z-axis), NOT vertical spacing — for a vertical/horizontal spacing layout use `Flex` (`layout/flex`; the aliases `vstack`/`hstack` resolve to it).

### auth

- [block/auth-login](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/auth-login.md) — Email + password login with remember-me checkbox and forgot-password link. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/auth-signup](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/auth-signup.md) — Full-name, email, password, confirm-password sign-up form with terms checkbox. _(copy-paste)_

### chart

- [chart/area-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/area-chart.md) — Area chart with optional stacking, multi-series support, and hover tooltip. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/bar-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/bar-chart.md) — Bar chart with vertical/horizontal orientation, grouped or stacked modes, and multi-series support. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/boxplot](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/boxplot.md) — Box-and-whisker plot with five-number summary and outlier dots per series. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/bubble-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/bubble-chart.md) — Bubble chart mapping x, y, and size dimensions; radius is area-proportional via sqrt scale. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/bullet](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/bullet.md) — Bullet chart with background range bands, measure bar, and target tick. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/calendar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/calendar.md) — Calendar heatmap — a week-column grid of day cells colored by value (GitHub-style). _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/candlestick](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/candlestick.md) — OHLC financial chart — each period a high–low wick and an open↔close body, coloured up/down. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/combo-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/combo-chart.md) — Combination bar + line chart on shared or dual y-axes. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/funnel](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/funnel.md) — Vertical conversion funnel — each stage is a trapezoid narrowing toward the next, with optional conversion labels. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/gauge](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/gauge.md) — A speedometer gauge — a value arc over a min–max sweep with threshold zones, ticks, and a needle. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/heatmap](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/heatmap.md) — Two-dimensional heatmap with band scales and color-mix cell interpolation. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/histogram](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/histogram.md) — Frequency histogram using Freedman–Diaconis binning with hover tooltips. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/kpi](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/kpi.md) — KPI card showing a primary metric with optional delta indicator, icon, and sparkline. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/line-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/line-chart.md) — Time-series or numeric line chart with multi-series support, hover tooltip, and legend. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/meter](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/meter.md) — Progress meter in bar or gauge variant with threshold coloring. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/pie-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/pie-chart.md) — Pie or donut chart with hover segments and optional legend. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/polar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/polar.md) — A polar coordinate plot — categories around the circle, value as radius. Bars (a rose), or a polar line/area. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/radar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/radar.md) — Radar / spider chart with polar grid rings, spokes, and multi-series polygon overlays. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/radial-bar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/radial-bar.md) — Concentric radial bars (a circular gauge family) — each datum is a ring whose sweep is proportional to its value. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/sankey](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/sankey.md) — Flow diagram — ranked nodes connected by throughput-sized link ribbons. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/scatter-chart](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/scatter-chart.md) — Scatter plot with variable point radius, multi-series, and hover tooltip. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/sparkline](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/sparkline.md) — Compact inline sparkline for embedding trend data in dashboards or KPI cards. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/stream](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/stream.md) — Streamgraph — stacked areas on a centered (silhouette) flowing baseline. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/sunburst](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/sunburst.md) — Radial hierarchy — concentric rings where each node is an annular segment sized by value. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_
- [chart/treemap](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chart/treemap.md) — Squarified treemap for visualizing part-to-whole hierarchical data. _(npm @cascivo/charts)_

### dashboard

- [block/dashboard-overview](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/dashboard-overview.md) — Welcome header with four KPI stat cards (revenue, users, orders, conversion). _(copy-paste)_
- [block/dashboard-table](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/dashboard-table.md) — Searchable, sortable, paginated data table with export button. _(copy-paste)_

### display

- [alert](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/alert.md) — Highlights a short, important message inline _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [avatar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/avatar.md) — Displays a user image with initials fallback _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [avatar-group](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/avatar-group.md) — Overlapping stack of avatars with a max cap and an i18n-labelled +N overflow chip _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [badge](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/badge.md) — Small status label or category indicator _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [block/console-app](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/console-app.md) — Carbon-parity console shell: ShellHeader + icon-rail SideNav + HeaderPanel notifications/switcher + collapsible aside + main content. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/dashboard-charts](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/dashboard-charts.md) — Dashboard layout with KPI tiles, line chart, bar chart, and pie chart. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/empty-dashboard](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/empty-dashboard.md) — Dashboard page showing an empty state with a call-to-action button. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/login-page](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/login-page.md) — Authentication login page with email and password form. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/notification-center](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/notification-center.md) — A list of notification alerts with a mark-all-read action. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/page-with-breadcrumb](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/page-with-breadcrumb.md) — A centered content page with a breadcrumb navigation and page header. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/settings-form-page](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/settings-form-page.md) — Settings page with profile form inside a two-column settings layout. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/sidebar-app](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/sidebar-app.md) — Full app shell with collapsible side navigation and top header. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/stats-cards](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/stats-cards.md) — Grid of KPI stat cards with trend badges. _(copy-paste)_
- [block/users-table-page](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/block/users-table-page.md) — Full users management page with table, search, and invite action. _(copy-paste)_
- [blockquote](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/blockquote.md) — Quoted passage with optional attribution footer _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [card](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/card.md) — Container for grouping related content _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [carousel](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/carousel.md) — Scroll-snap slide deck with previous/next controls and dot indicators _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [chat-bubble](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/chat-bubble.md) — Message bubble for chat and messaging UIs with avatar, name, and timestamp support _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [code](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/code.md) — Inline code span for identifiers, commands, and short snippets _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [code-snippet](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/code-snippet.md) — Displays code (inline, single-line, or multi-line) with an optional copy button, lightweight built-in syntax highlighting for bash/css/js/ts, and an optional terminal-window look _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [collapsible](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/collapsible.md) — A single disclosure region toggled open and closed by its trigger _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [comparison](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/comparison.md) — Reveals the difference between two layers with a draggable divider _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [contained-list](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/contained-list.md) — Labelled list of rows inside a bordered container _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [data-list](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/data-list.md) — Key-value pairs rendered as a description list _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [data-table](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/data-table.md) — Signal-driven data table with client/server sort, filter, pagination, multi-selection, expandable rows, and CSS content-visibility row containment for large datasets _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [editor/highlight](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/editor/highlight.md) — Read-only syntax-highlighted code block — the same owned tokenizer as CodeEditor, without the textarea. _(npm @cascivo/editor)_
- [empty-state](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/empty-state.md) — Placeholder for views that have no data to display _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [flow/flow](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow.md) — The declarative, AI-first flow surface — render a node/edge graph from plain serializable data. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-background](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-background.md) — Decorative dots / grid / cross canvas background, drawn purely in CSS gradients. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-canvas](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-canvas.md) — The pan/zoom canvas pane — a single CSS-transformed layer driven by the viewport signal. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-controls](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-controls.md) — Zoom in / out / fit-view controls for a flow canvas — real, i18n-labeled buttons. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-edge](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-edge.md) — An SVG edge with bezier/straight/smoothstep paths, an arrowhead, an optional label, and animation. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-handle](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-handle.md) — A connection port on a node edge — where edges attach and interactive connect starts. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-minimap](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-minimap.md) — A scaled SVG overview of the graph with a draggable viewport rectangle. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-node](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-node.md) — An HTML node box positioned in the viewport pane — draggable, selectable, with arbitrary children. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-panel](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-panel.md) — An absolutely-positioned slot for custom flow-canvas UI (legend, toolbar). _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [flow/flow-story](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/flow/flow-story.md) — A scripted, sequenced, looping flow animation — walks a graph step by step with fade-in captions. _(npm @cascivo/flow)_
- [heading](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/heading.md) — Section heading with semantic level decoupled from visual size _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [image](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/image.md) — Image with load state, blur-up placeholder, graceful fallback, and optional zoom _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [item](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/item.md) — Generic content row primitive with media, content, and action regions _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [kbd](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/kbd.md) — Displays a keyboard key or shortcut _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [list](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/list.md) — Styled unordered or ordered list with ListItem _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [log-viewer](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/log-viewer.md) — Virtualized monospace console for high-frequency log and stream output _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [prose](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/prose.md) — Wrapper that styles raw descendant HTML — headings, lists, code, quotes, tables _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [qr-code](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/qr-code.md) — Encodes a URL or short text into a scannable SVG QR code _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [relative-time](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/relative-time.md) — Displays a date as a localized phrase relative to now, auto-updating _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [separator](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/separator.md) — Visual or semantic divider between content _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [skeleton](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/skeleton.md) — Animated loading placeholder that mirrors the shape of pending content _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [stat](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/stat.md) — Displays a key metric with optional delta, trend direction and help text _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [status](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/status.md) — Colored dot with a label communicating the state of a system or entity _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [structured-list](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/structured-list.md) — Tabular row list for scannable data, optionally single-selectable _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [swipe-item](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/swipe-item.md) — List row whose leading/trailing actions are revealed by a horizontal swipe, with keyboard parity _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [tag](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/tag.md) — Compact chip for labeling, categorizing, or filtering content _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [text](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/text.md) — Body text with size, weight, and muted variants _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [timeline](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/timeline.md) — Ordered sequence of events with status markers and a connector line _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [tree-view](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/tree-view.md) — Hierarchical, expandable tree of nodes with keyboard navigation and selection _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [user](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/user.md) — Identity composite: an avatar with a name, description, and optional action slot _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [visually-hidden](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/visually-hidden.md) — Hides content visually while keeping it available to screen readers _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_

### feedback

- [inline-loading](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/inline-loading.md) — Compact inline status indicator that pairs a label with a loading, success, or error state _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [notification](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/notification.md) — Inline, actionable notification banner that surfaces a titled message with an optional recovery action _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [progress](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/progress.md) — Horizontal bar showing the completion progress of a tracked operation _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [progress-bar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/progress-bar.md) — Shows determinate or indeterminate progress of a task _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [progress-circle](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/progress-circle.md) — Circular determinate progress indicator rendered as an SVG arc _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [pull-to-refresh](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/pull-to-refresh.md) — Wraps a scrollable region and triggers a refresh when pulled down past a threshold at the top _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [radial-progress](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/radial-progress.md) — Circular progress indicator using conic-gradient, with percentage label and variant colors _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [spinner](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/spinner.md) — Indeterminate loading indicator _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_

### inputs

- [button](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/button.md) — Triggers an action or event _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [button-group](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/button-group.md) — Visually joins a set of related buttons into a single segmented control _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [calendar](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/calendar.md) — An accessible standalone month-grid date picker. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [checkbox](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/checkbox.md) — Binary toggle for forms, with indeterminate support _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [checkbox-card](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/checkbox-card.md) — Multi-selectable card backed by a native checkbox. Use multiple independent CheckboxCards for multi-select scenarios. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [color-picker](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/color-picker.md) — Interactive color selection widget with saturation/lightness area, hue and alpha sliders _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [combobox](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/combobox.md) — Filterable single-select with an animated custom listbox, built on the dropdown open/close machine _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [copy-button](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/copy-button.md) — Icon button that copies a value to the clipboard with copied feedback _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [date-picker](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/date-picker.md) — An accessible date-picker with a calendar popover. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [date-range-picker](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/date-range-picker.md) — A dual-calendar picker for selecting a contiguous date range. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [editable](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/editable.md) — Inline click-to-edit text field _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [editor/code-editor](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/editor/code-editor.md) — Lightweight code editor — a native textarea overlaid on a syntax-highlighted layer, with line numbers and Tab indent. _(npm @cascivo/editor)_
- [fab](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/fab.md) — Floating action button anchored to a screen corner, with an optional speed-dial of secondary actions _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [field](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/field.md) — Form-field wrapper composing label, control, description, and error _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [file-uploader](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/file-uploader.md) — Drag-and-drop file upload zone with file list and status indicators. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [filter](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/filter.md) — A group of toggleable pill or outline buttons for filtering content by category _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [form](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/form.md) — Typed signal-based form store (createForm/useForm) with sync/async validation and a thin Form element wrapper _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [icon-button](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/icon-button.md) — Square, icon-only button with a required accessible label _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [input](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/input.md) — Text input field with optional label, hint, and error state _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [input-group](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/input-group.md) — Prefix/suffix addon wrapper for Input; InputGroupAddon renders inline icons/units inside the field border; ButtonGroup collapses adjacent button borders _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [label](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/label.md) — Accessible caption for a form control _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [multi-select](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/multi-select.md) — Searchable multi-value select with popover listbox _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [native-select](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/native-select.md) — A styled native <select> that keeps platform form/keyboard behavior with a custom chevron and focus ring _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [number-input](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/number-input.md) — Numeric input with stepper buttons, clamping, precision, and locale formatting _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [otp-input](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/otp-input.md) — Segmented one-time code input _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [password-input](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/password-input.md) — Password input with reveal toggle and optional strength meter _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [radio](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/radio.md) — Single choice from a set, grouped with RadioGroup _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [radio-card](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/radio-card.md) — Selectable card backed by a native radio input. Use RadioCardGroup for single-select groups. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [rating-group](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/rating-group.md) — Star rating input with accessible radio group pattern _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [search](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/search.md) — Search input with debounced search callback and clear button _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [segmented-control](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/segmented-control.md) — Mutually exclusive toggle group _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [select](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/select.md) — Native select menu styled to match the design system _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [slider](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/slider.md) — Range input for selecting a value within bounds _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [swap](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/swap.md) — Animated toggle between two icon/content states with rotate or flip transition _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [tags-input](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/tags-input.md) — Free-form multi-value chip input _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [textarea](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/textarea.md) — Multi-line text input with optional label, hint, and error state _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [tile](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/tile.md) — A selectable card with radio (single) or checkbox (multi) semantics, toggled by click or keyboard _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [time-picker](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/time-picker.md) — Native time input wrapper with label, hint, error, and size variants _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [toggle](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/toggle.md) — On/off switch built as an accessible button _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [toggle-group](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/toggle-group.md) — A set of toggle buttons for single or multiple selection _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_

### layout

- [app-shell](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/app-shell.md) — Sticky-header + full-height side-nav + single-scroll-container layout with an animated, accessible nav toggle. _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [aspect-ratio](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/aspect-ratio.md) — Constrains content to a fixed width-to-height ratio _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [indicator](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/indicator.md) — Positions an overlay element (badge, dot, count) at a corner of its child _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [join](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/join.md) — Groups adjacent children into a seamless joined element by removing interior borders and radii _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [layout/app-shell](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/app-shell.md) — Full-page application shell with persisted collapsible sidebar. Includes a signal-driven top progress bar with determinate progress, error state, and dismissible error strip. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/auth-layout](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/auth-layout.md) — Centered card layout for authentication pages (login, register, forgot password). _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/auto-grid](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/auto-grid.md) — Media-query-free responsive grid — columns auto-fill based on available space. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/center](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/center.md) — Horizontally centered container with a configurable max-width. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/columns](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/columns.md) — Equal-width multi-column layout that collapses to single column on narrow viewports. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/dashboard-layout](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/dashboard-layout.md) — Dashboard page layout with stats strip, main content area, and optional aside. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/flex](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/flex.md) — Flex layout primitive for vertical or horizontal stacking with gap control. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/grid](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/grid.md) — CSS grid layout primitive with responsive column collapsing. Establishes its own containment, so responsive `cols` adapt to the grid’s own slot width with no wrapper or container ancestor required. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/masonry](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/masonry.md) — Masonry layout — native CSS masonry where supported, multi-column fallback elsewhere (fallback orders items top-to-bottom per column). _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/page-header](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/page-header.md) — Page-level header with title, description, breadcrumb, and actions slots. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/section](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/section.md) — Page-section shell with block padding, centered inner width, and stack gap. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/settings-layout](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/settings-layout.md) — Two-column settings page layout with a fixed-width menu and fluid content area. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/spacer](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/spacer.md) — Fixed-height spacing block using design token steps. _(copy-paste)_
- [layout/split-view](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/layout/split-view.md) — Resizable two-pane split layout with keyboard and pointer drag support. _(copy-paste)_
- [resizable](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/resizable.md) — Two-pane splitter whose divider can be dragged or keyboard-nudged to reallocate space _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [scroll-area](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/scroll-area.md) — A scroll container with styled, slim scrollbars and overflow shadows _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
- [section/cta](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/cta.md) — Call-to-action band — quiet hairline-bordered section with title, description, and centered actions. Replace demo content before shipping. _(copy-paste)_
- [section/feature-grid](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/feature-grid.md) — Feature section — AutoGrid of items with optional title, description, and icon slots. Icons are optional; the grid works text-only. Replace demo content before shipping. _(copy-paste)_
- [section/hero](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/hero.md) — Page hero section — centered or split layout with eyebrow, title, description, actions and media slots. Replace demo content before shipping. _(copy-paste)_
- [section/media-masonry](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/media-masonry.md) — Masonry gallery section — native CSS masonry where supported, multi-column fallback elsewhere (fallback orders tiles top-to-bottom per column). Tiles style themselves; section provides only the layout shell. _(copy-paste)_
- [section/page-footer](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/page-footer.md) — Site footer — AutoGrid of link groups with a brand/meta bottom row. Renders a <footer> element with a <nav aria-label="Footer"> wrapping the link columns. _(copy-paste)_
- [section/stats-band](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/section/stats-band.md) — KPI strip — horizontal band of stats with optional delta and inline sparkline trend. Wraps via AutoGrid on narrow containers. No visible heading; provide aria-label for accessibility. _(copy-paste)_
- [stack](https://docs.cascivo.com/llms/stack.md) — Overlaps children in a CSS grid stack with a configurable offset to create a card-pile effect _(npm @cascivo/react · or copy-paste)_
